 {"id":"br-97gc6.1.3","title":"Control plane: define bug intake, closure, and reopen rules for discovered defects","description":"Specify when a found defect gets its own bug bead, when it can be closed as part of a parent audit bead, what verification proof is required, and when a previously closed item must be reopened after a regression or conflicting evidence.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T22:53:20.626743612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:17:55.041367461Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:17:55.041067799Z","close_reason":"Completed: defined bug intake rules (separate beads for non-trivial, multi-subsystem, security, or blocked issues), closure rules (trivial fixes inline, verified in same session), verification proof (rch output, test pass, before/after evidence), and reopen triggers (regression, incomplete fix, flawed verification).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","process","tracking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T22:53:20.626743612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:12:41.635658627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1378,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.1.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Bug Intake, Closure, and Reopen Rules

### When a Defect Gets Its Own Bug Bead
- Non-trivial bugs requiring investigation or multiple changes
- Bugs affecting multiple subsystems or crates
- Security or data integrity issues (always separate for audit trail)
- Issues discovered but not immediately fixable (blocked by upstream, rch, etc.)
- Regressions in previously verified behavior

### When a Defect Can Be Closed Within Parent Audit Bead
- Trivial one-liner fixes (typos, copy-paste, obvious omissions)
- Self-contained issues fixed in same commit as audit pass
- Issues where fix + verification happen in same session with clear evidence
- Compiler/lint issues with immediate clean output

### Verification Proof Required
- **Compiler/lint fixes**: clean cargo check/clippy output (via rch)
- **Runtime bugs**: targeted test output showing pass
- **Behavior changes**: before/after evidence or test delta
- **Blocked verification**: explicit blocker provenance, command attempted, and residual-risk note

### When to Reopen a Closed Bug
- Regression in subsequent changes (new evidence contradicts closure)
- Original fix was incomplete (missed edge cases)
- Verification was flawed or used stale/mock evidence
- Conflicting evidence from sibling audit lane

### Audit Trail Requirement
Every closure must cite: files affected, fix summary, verification command or test name, and any residual risk.

Recovery artifacts remain preserved pending explicit approval.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","db","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:06:42.249742525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1260,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Evidence on 2026-03-18: the live anomaly is specific to .beads/beads.db-wal existing without .beads/beads.db-shm. After the long-running br writer exited, lsof showed no remaining open handles on beads.db or beads.db-wal, but the WAL file still persisted. Preserved recovery artifacts are separate from that live complaint: .beads/.br_recovery contains beads.db.20260313_224031_514673114.bak and beads.db-wal.orphan_20260313T224229Z, while the repo root also contains historical beads.db.bad_*, beads.db.rebuild_*, vacuum/reindex test files, and recovery_20260313T224002Z.","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:44:48Z"},{"id":1265,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"TopazIbis","text":"Evidence on 2026-03-19: `am macros start-session` initially failed in `/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust` because repo-local `./storage.sqlite3` was malformed and had a stale `storage.sqlite3-wal` sidecar without a live repo-local holder. `lsof storage.sqlite3 storage.sqlite3-wal` in the repo showed no open handles. The unhealthy listener on port 8765 is a separate process (`/home/ubuntu/.local/bin/am`, pid 3943291, cwd `/home/ubuntu`) holding `/home/ubuntu/storage.sqlite3` and `/home/ubuntu/storage.sqlite3-wal`, so it is not the writer for the repo-local DB. Safe non-destructive remediation for the repo-local mailbox DB was: 1) verify no repo-local open handles, 2) run `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am doctor reconstruct --dry-run --json`, 3) run `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli am doctor reconstruct --yes --json`. That quarantined the corrupt DB to `storage.sqlite3.corrupt-*` artifacts and rebuilt a fresh `./storage.sqlite3` from the Git mailbox archive, after which `am doctor check` reported the DB accessible and I could register as `TopazIbis`. Residual findings after reconstruct: archive/db parity still fails (`messages archive=1951 db=1292`), `schema_version` is `user_version = 0`, the archive contains duplicate canonical message ids and missing `project.json` files for some archived projects, and the repo now has preserved `storage.sqlite3*.corrupt-*` / salvage artifacts by design rather than active live-state debris. Separate but related tracker evidence: `.beads/beads.db-wal` still exists without `.beads/beads.db-shm`, `br doctor` still reports that anomaly, and `br update` for this bead persisted the status change to `in_progress` but returned `UNIQUE constraint failed: blocked_issues_cache.issue_id`, so Beads mutation reporting needs separate follow-up.","created_at":"2026-03-19T17:47:01Z"},{"id":1268,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"TopazIbis","text":"Patched `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs` to handle the critical mailbox split where `am mail inbox` could fail with `Resource temporarily busy` while robot reads still succeeded. The fix adds a minimal inbox-read schema probe, a best-effort direct SQLite fallback for mailbox reads, routes `mail inbox` through that fallback when the async pool/init path reports a transient busy error, and covers the read-path with new CLI tests. Remote verification is running through `rch`; one unrelated pre-existing async-closure lifetime issue in `get_project_record()` had to be flattened first so the crate could compile. Attempted to notify other agents via Agent Mail, but the live server on `127.0.0.1:8765` still rejects `TopazIbis` because it is pointed at a different mailbox DB than the repaired repo-local one.","created_at":"2026-03-19T18:10:16Z"},{"id":1269,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"TopazIbis","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up on the mailbox fallback patch uncovered two more real issues in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs`: (1) `check_inbox_direct()` was still using a weaker manual project lookup than the new direct inbox path, so it now reuses `context::resolve_project()`; (2) the fallback trigger initially missed real DB-layer variants (`Resource temporarily busy` / raw `database is busy`) and then became too broad, so `is_resource_busy_cli_error()` is now narrowed to transient busy/lock forms only. Added unit coverage for the accepted busy variants and a negative assertion for non-transient `disk i/o error`.","created_at":"2026-03-19T19:23:14Z"},{"id":1270,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fresh-eyes pass: fixed another inbox fallback hole in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs. classify_server_tool_call/server_tool_error_is_unavailable now treats HTTP 5xx from the MCP server as unavailable rather than hard rejection, so am mail inbox can still fall back to the local DB path when the server is unhealthy. Added tests covering 5xx fallback and preserving 4xx rejection semantics. Re-running rch cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --tests; expected remaining blocker is still the unrelated upstream sqlmodel-frankensqlite compile failure.","created_at":"2026-03-19T19:31:14Z"},{"id":1271,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fresh-eyes pass: fixed another CLI mailbox inconsistency in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs. am mail inbox now validates --limit before any server/async/direct path, so invalid limits no longer behave differently depending on whether fallback triggers. Added unit tests for validate_mail_inbox_limit. Latest rch cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --tests still fails outside this slice due to unrelated workspace errors in sqlmodel-frankensqlite (Arc<str>/Arc<[u8]> conversions) and mcp-agent-mail-core/src/atc_admissibility.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-19T19:40:20Z"},{"id":1272,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Random deep-dive pass: traced share/export pipeline (CLI -> snapshot -> scrub -> bundle -> finalize) for context, then focused on mcp-agent-mail-core ATC modules because atc_admissibility.rs contained a broken denial-code path. Fixed evaluate_admissibility to map failed gates to the actual DenialCode enum via a dedicated helper, added coverage for non-high-risk exploration budget exhaustion, and removed an unused variable in atc_voi_control.rs. Verification via rch: cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-core --lib --tests = OK; cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-core --lib atc_admissibility -- --nocapture = 14 passed.","created_at":"2026-03-19T19:46:16Z"},{"id":1273,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Deep fellow-agent review pass: fixed two concrete regressions and re-validated touched slices. (1) crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/atc_admissibility.rs had a broken denial path using an undefined variable and nonexistent DenialCode variants; replaced it with explicit gate->denial mapping and added low-risk exploration-budget coverage. (2) crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_compose.rs still treated char indices as terminal cell widths after the Unicode subject-count fix; wide characters could misplace cursors and overflow bordered inputs. Added width-aware truncation/cursor math and a regression test. Also tightened body byte-limit enforcement so multibyte chars cannot push drafts past MAX_BODY_LEN during typing. Reviewed current tui_bridge/tui_events diffs too; no new obvious defect there. Verification: rch cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-core --lib --tests = OK; rch cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-core --lib atc_admissibility -- --nocapture = 14 passed; rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on touched files = OK. Server rch checks remain blocked by upstream sqlmodel-frankensqlite Arc<str>/Arc<[u8]> type errors, not by these local changes.","created_at":"2026-03-19T19:55:14Z"},{"id":1274,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Additional fresh-eyes pass: found and fixed another TUI compose regression in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_compose.rs. Long multi-line drafts could move the body cursor off-screen because body_scroll existed in state but render never reconciled it with body_cursor_line. Added effective_body_scroll(body_rows, line_count), used it during body rendering, and added focused tests for cursor-visible scroll behavior. rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on the updated file = OK. rch targeted server tests were re-run, but verification is still blocked by the same unrelated upstream server build dependency path / sqlmodel-frankensqlite compile failure rather than by this local compose change.","created_at":"2026-03-19T20:00:08Z"},{"id":1275,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Deep-review pass in reconstruct salvage path: fixed message merge so salvaged messages rebuild normalized message_recipients from messages.recipients_json instead of depending on message_recipients table survival; canonicalized malformed/variant recipients_json during salvage; and removed message/recipient-table early returns so malformed salvage tables no longer abort the rest of the merge. Added regression test reconstruct_with_salvage_rebuilds_recipients_when_recipient_table_is_missing. Verification: rustfmt --check on crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/reconstruct.rs passed; rch exec -- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db reconstruct_with_salvage -- --nocapture passed (3 salvage tests green); broader rch exec -- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-db --lib --tests still blocked by upstream sqlmodel-frankensqlite Arc<str>/Arc<[u8]> mismatches.","created_at":"2026-03-19T20:27:58Z"},{"id":1375,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.3.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Final diagnosis summary (2026-04-02):

WAL-without-SHM: This is *expected frankensqlite behavior*, not an anomaly. The warning explicitly says "(expected for frankensqlite)". The WAL file is 32 bytes (header only with magic 0x377f0682), indicating no pending transactions. Frankensqlite doesn't use SHM files for coordination like C SQLite does.

Never-used pages: integrity_check reports 29+ "never used" pages (53, 54, 263, 1107, etc.). This is database bloat from past delete/update operations, NOT read-blocking corruption. The database is fully readable with 1923 issues. A VACUUM would reclaim this space if needed, but it's non-blocking.

Preserved recovery artifacts: .br_recovery/ contains 2 files from March 13, 2026 recovery operations. The root directory has additional .bad_* and .rebuild_* artifacts from March 12, 2026. All are safely quarantined historical evidence.

Current state: No open file handles on beads.db or its WAL. DB-vs-JSONL count difference (1923 vs 1702) is expected since JSONL is the canonical source and DB may contain additional cached state.

Remediation: Child beads documented the safe policy. No action required during normal operation. The br doctor warnings are informational, not blocking.

 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","title":"Durability program: unify archive/DB reconciliation ownership behind a mailbox supervisor","description":"Create one authoritative control plane for mailbox health, degraded reads, and recovery ownership.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:02.410545746Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:06:22.297863597Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:06:22.297664375Z","close_reason":"Implemented in durability program work","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","db","recovery","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:02.410545746Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1278,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Intent

Build a single authoritative control plane for mailbox health, drift handling, degraded reads, and recovery.

Today, too many entrypoints can still make slightly different decisions about whether SQLite is healthy, stale, or recoverable. That spreads correctness logic across startup checks, doctor paths, CLI opens, server reads, and helper layers.

This issue consolidates those decisions behind one mailbox-supervisor model:
- one authoritative health verdict
- one ownership boundary for mutation
- one degraded-read policy
- one candidate-based recovery engine

## Why It Matters

If this bead is underspecified, the rest of the durability work collapses back into scattered local fixes. This is the architectural core of the subtree.

## Deliverables

- explicit mailbox state machine and invariants
- centralized health verdict computation
- split-brain/deleted-binary detection
- degraded read-only snapshot policy
- exclusive candidate recovery and atomic promotion
- recovery receipts/stamps and upgrade-safe supervisor lifecycle

## Acceptance Shape

Every entrypoint should ultimately rely on the same mailbox-health verdict and recovery engine. Read surfaces should stop making independent mutation decisions. Deleted-binary and stale-owner cases should be first-class failure signals rather than odd process trivia.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.1","title":"Specify mailbox durability states, transitions, and invariants","description":"Define the authoritative mailbox durability state machine and ownership invariants.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:02.417591410Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T00:36:53.141881148Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T00:36:53.141213748Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:02.417591410Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1281,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"States to define explicitly: `Healthy`, `Stale`, `Suspect`, `Broken`, `Recovering`, `DegradedReadOnly`, and `Escalate`.

This bead should answer:
- what evidence moves the mailbox into each state
- which transitions are allowed
- when reads may continue
- when writes must stop
- when exclusive recovery is required
- which layer owns the transition decision

The goal is to stop CLI/server/doctor implementations from inventing their own semantics.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:02Z"},{"id":1379,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.1.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Specification complete: created docs/SPEC-mailbox-durability-states.md

The spec defines:
- 7 canonical states: Healthy, Stale, Suspect, Broken, Recovering, DegradedReadOnly, Escalate
- State transition rules and forbidden transitions
- Evidence mapping (what observations trigger which state)
- Operation semantics by state (reads/writes allowed)
- Ownership rules (DB layer owns verdict computation)
- CLI/server behavior by state
- Testing requirements per br-97gc6.5.2.6.1.1

Key decisions:
- Stale threshold: >5% or >100 message count mismatch
- WAL-without-SHM for frankensqlite is Suspect (documented benign), not Broken
- "Never used" pages from integrity_check are Suspect (bloat), not Broken
- Recovery requires exclusive lock with PID tracking for stale detection
- Escalate triggers after 3 failed recovery attempts or corrupted archive
","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:18:03Z"}]}

Key components:
- MailboxState enum with 7 canonical states (Healthy, Stale, Suspect, Broken, Recovering, DegradedReadOnly, Escalate)
- MailboxVerdict struct with complete evidence trail
- ProbeResult and ProbeSeverity types
- VerdictOptions with fast() preset for hot-path checks
- compute_mailbox_verdict() entry point running layered probes

Probes implemented:
1. probe_db_file_exists - checks file presence and non-zero size
2. probe_db_file_sanity - quick_check via sqlite_file_is_healthy
3. probe_archive_accessible - read_dir on archive root
4. probe_archive_writable - write probe file test
5. probe_sidecar_state - WAL/SHM presence and pairing
6. probe_recovery_lock - lock file with PID and liveness check
7. probe_archive_db_parity - count comparison with configurable thresholds

State computation:
- Severity-based: Escalate > Broken > Recovering > Suspect > Stale > DegradedReadOnly > Healthy
- Recovery lock takes precedence when held
- Stale vs Suspect determined by probe name heuristics

Exports added to lib.rs:
- MailboxState, MailboxVerdict, ProbeResult, ProbeSeverity, VerdictOptions, compute_mailbox_verdict

 {"id":"br-97gc6.5.2.3","title":"Durability program: capture forensic incident bundles before repair/reconstruct","description":"Capture structured forensic bundles before any repair, reconstruct, or automatic recovery mutates mailbox state.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:03.742521002Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:06:22.312929593Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:06:22.312713428Z","close_reason":"Implemented in durability program work","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","db","doctor","forensics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.3","depends_on_id":"br-97gc6.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:01:03.742521002Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1280,"issue_id":"br-97gc6.5.2.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Intent

Preserve enough evidence to explain why a mailbox entered recovery before any quarantine, repair, reconstruct, or candidate promotion step mutates the database family.

The system should not force operators to choose between restoring service and preserving diagnosable evidence.

## Bundle Doctrine

A forensic bundle is a first-class product artifact. It should capture:
- the live DB family and sidecars
- archive drift facts
- lock/process state
- environment/config summary
- candidate validation outputs

## Why This Matters

The forensic bundle is the bridge between invisible self-healing and later root-cause analysis. Without it, every incident turns back into guesswork.
","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:07:02Z"}]}
